title: coreopsis grandiflora var. saxicola
intro: Coreopsis grandiflora var. saxicola, also known as Rough-leaf Coreopsis, is a variety of flowering plant in the aster family, characterized by its bright yellow daisy-like blooms and coarse, hairy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Coreopsis grandiflora var. saxicola include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a useful herb for treating a range of health conditions. Coreopsis grandiflora var. saxicola can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
